programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan kick things off with their starters. Johnnie makes foie gras ice cream, cherry ravioli and iberico ham; Aiden offers black cherry and foie gras terrine with palm sugar mousse; and Simons entry is grilled salad, truffle custard, cheese foam and cobnut crisp.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan present their respective fish courses. Johnnie concocts a Recreation of the Sea; Aiden makes beetroot poached salmon, caviar, razor clam, citrus and fennel salad; and Simon serves up lobster with pickled beetroot, sweet apple and cuckoo flower.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan are challenged to cook their mains and Marcus Wareing judges the dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan present their respective desserts. Its the final chance for the chefs to impress veteran Marcus Wareing as he decides who will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs compete for the chance to cook at a glittering Olympic banquet. The North West finalists go head-to-head as they prepare a lavish meal for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In the battle of the London and South East chefs newcomers Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their starter menus. Will it be Phil with spring salad with goats milk puree, pickled asparagus and quails eggs; Graham with pickled rabbit, wild garlic quinoa and frozen rabbit liver parfait; or Marcus with iced lambs liver parfait, malt loaf, fingerling limes and rose geranium?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomers Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their fish courses. Will it be Phil with a tasting of Cornish mackerel with oysters, mussels, winkles and samphire; Marcus with pollock, peas, coconut and elderflower; or Graham with octopus, blood orange, feta cheese, squid ink tuile and black olive salt?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness cook their main courses, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Phil with roast loin of lamb with pie and mash, carrots, nettles and mint; Graham with slow cooked marsh lamb breast, rasher and spiced rissole; or Marcus with blade of beef cooked in hay, tendons and beetroot?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their dessert dishes here. Will it be Phil with rhubarb and custard souffle, Marcus with asparagus, goats curd and black olive, or Graham with lemon meringue? Veteran Jason Atherton decides who goes through to the judges and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from the London and South East region. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do. The chefs task is to create a menu which captures the Olympic spirit.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle of the Wales chefs: returning contenders Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry. All are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. Stephen Terry cooks `The Opening Ceremony, Richard Davies makes ravioli of quail, parfait of foie gras, roasted salsify and Madeira jelly, and James Sommerin cooks sage cream, onion, chicken and Welsh `Brie truffle toast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry are all determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. They cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be Stephen Terry with `The Fish and Shellfish Medley, Richard Davies with compression of vine tomato, poached lobster and hand dived scallop, or James Sommerin with lobster and Iberico ham, spiced butter and broccoli?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry cook their main course, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Stephen Terry with `The Bunny Pentathlon, Richard Davies with slow cooked rib of beef, beetroot ketchup, bone marrow and braised shallot, or James Sommerin with mangalitsa pork, carrot, liquorice and leek?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry cook their dessert, but who will impress? Stephen Terry with `Bronze, Silver or Gold?, Richard Davies with passion fruit cream and gel with bitter chocolate sorbet or James Sommerin with raspberry and lemon cheesecake? `Great British Menu veteran Angela Hartnett decides who goes through to the judges on Friday and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from Wales. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: South West contenders Paul Ainsworth and Nathan Outlaw, plus newcomer Simon Hulstone, kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be Paul with his `Breakfast of Champions; Nathan with hogs pudding with seaweed, potato terrine and mushroom ketchup; or Simon Hulstone with track and field - beetroot and goats cheese?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their fish course, but which one but which one will prevail? Will it be Paul Ainsworth with monkfish `Two Showings; Nathan Outlaw with mackerel, horseradish and oyster sauce; or Simon Hulstone with `The Swimming Pool?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their main course, but which chef will take gold? Will it be Paul Ainsworth with `Wishful Chicken; Nathan Outlaw with duck, barbecue monkfish, rosemary, samphire and asparagus; or Simon Hulstone with lamb with salted toffee pea mousse, shallot puree and asparagus?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs final chance to impress the veteran with their dessert dishes. Will it be Paul Ainsworth with `Then and Now; Nathan Outlaw with elderflower and lemon tart, with strawberry sorbet and meringues; or Simon Hulstone with `Closing Ceremony?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the South West region. One chef left the competition on the previous day and only two remain to cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The 2012 competition enters its final week. The winners of the regional heats compete for a chance for their dish to be on the final banquet menu at a prestigious event hosted by Sir Steve Redgrave with a glittering guest list of British Olympians. Here, the finalists cook their starters, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Richard Corrigan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The eight winners of the regional heats prepare to cook their fish dishes, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Marcus Wareing. Before they get into the kitchen, they must first learn whether they will all be cooking.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: On day three, the chosen finalists cook their main courses, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Tom Kerridge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its desserts day in the finals, and the last chance for the competing chefs to impress the judges and veteran Angela Hartnett. After a hard-fought week, the end of this programme will see the judges finally reveal the winning four dishes which will make up this years `Great British Menu.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This final programme follows the successful chefs in the run-up to the Olympic banquet at the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, as they make the painstaking preparations for what is one of the most important meals they will ever cook. The four winning chefs prepare an outstanding four-course menu for a truly Olympian guest list.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline ODonnell and Neil Rankin go head to head to represent Scotland. Today they must impress the mystery veteran judge with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs, double Michelin starred Stevie McLaughlin, experienced Jacqueline ODonnell and newcomer Neil Rankin fight it out for the fish course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course for Scottish chefs Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline ODonnell and Neil Rankin. With the scores close, the pressure is on for all three and theyre each hoping to impress with unique and individual dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the Scottish chefs fates hang in the balance as at the end of today, one of them must be sent home. For Stevie McLaughlin, Jacqueline ODonnell and Neil Rankin theres no room for error.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs fight it out for just one place in the finals, cooking all four of their dishes again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Also scoring the menus is guest judge and guest of honour Jim Radford.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the South Wests finest chefs, returning contender Emily Watkins, experienced Dominic Chapman and newcomer Josh Eggleton fight it out. Today they must cook their starter courses to be judged by the surprise veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs, returner Emily Watkins and Michelin starred Dominic Chapman and Josh Eggleton do battle for the fish course. Dom has been inspired by his research trip to the scene of the D Day landings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs Emily Watkins, Dominic Chapman and Josh Eggleton cook their main courses for the veteran judge. With the competition so tight can one of them pull out in front before the deciding dessert course?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the South West chefs last chance to secure a place in the regional final. Two will make it through to cook their menus for the judges but either Emily Watkins, Dominic Chapman or Josh Eggleton must leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two South West chefs left standing cook their four courses again for the judges. Theyre joined by special guest judge and hero of the D-Day landings, 93-year-old war veteran Ken Sturdy, a Royal Navy signalman in World War II.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Wales top chefs, returning contender Mary Ann Gilchrist, former military man David Kelman and fellow newcomer Andy Beaumont compete for the last remaining place in the finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs, newcomer Andy Beaumont, military man David Kelman and returning contender Mary Ann Gilchrist go head to head for the fish course. Ambitious Andy and determined David are hoping to steel a lead over Mary Ann.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs Andy Beaumont, David Kelman and Mary Ann Gilchrist are all hoping to score highly with their very individual main courses. Mary Anns taking a risk with another austerity themed dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the Welsh chefs last chance to impress. After David Kelmans main course result, Mary Ann Gilchrist and Andy Beaumont must do everything they can not to crack under the pressure.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Welsh chefs fight it out for the last place in the finals. They must both cook all four courses of their D Day inspired menus again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge Martin Bell.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Northern Irelands top chefs, returning finalist Raymond McArdle, Richard Corrigans protege Chris McGowan and young gun Will Brown go head to head. They must impress a mystery veteran judge with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Northern Irish chefs, returning finalist Raymond McArdle, Richard Corrigans protege Chris McGowan and young gun Will Brown fight it out for the fish course. Rays hoping to build on his strong starter score.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day for the Northern Irish chefs. Rays going all out to impress the veteran judge by cooking two separate dishes but will last years Northern Irish champ let the pressure get to him?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the Northern Irish chefs last chance to impress with their dessert courses. Young gun Will Brown needs to play a blinder and hope that his rivals fall at the final hurdle as today one chef must leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Northern Irish chefs cook their entire menus again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort who are joined by special guest judge Celia Sandys.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the North Easts finest chefs, Michelin trained Paul Welburn, Michelin starred Frances Atkins and returning champion Colin McGurran will cook their menus for the mystery veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs Michelin starred Frances Atkins, determined Paul Welburn and returning champion Colin McGurran go head to head with their fish courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs Frances Atkins, Paul Welburn and Colin McGurran attempt to impress the veteran judge with their main courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress with their desserts as only two will get to cook for the judges. Frances Atkins serves another dish inspired by her fathers involvement in D Day.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs for the North East fight it out for one place in the finals, cooking all four of their courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis must do everything they can to impress the surprise veteran judge wholl be scoring their dishes with their starter courses and theyre each drawing on personal inspiration.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mary Ellen McTague, Jason Athertons protege James Durrant and fellow newcomer Mark Ellis go head to head for the fish course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day, and after the disastrous fish course Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis are all desperate to impress the veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis last chance to impress with their desserts as at the end of todays cooking one chef must leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort, who must decide which chef will win a place in the finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of London and the South Easts superstar chefs, returning contender Adam Simmonds, Michelin-starred newcomer Tom Sellers and his former boss Adam Byatt, attempt to dazzle the mystery judge with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: London and South East chefs, Adam Simmonds, Tom Sellers and Adam Byatt continue their battle. Does a trip to Adam Byatts nans house to find out about his grandfathers military connections spur him on to fish course glory?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The London and South East chefs Adam Simmonds, Tom Sellers and Adam Byatt go head to head cooking their main courses. At the crucial half way stage the chefs must do all they can to score highly.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert course time as the London and South East chefs compete for one of two places in the judges chamber and the competition couldnt be tighter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a tightly fought battle today as the two remaining London and South East chefs cook all four of their dishes again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge, Joy Hunter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central regions top chefs, returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett go into battle. They begin with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett fight it out for fish course glory. After plating up late last time will Aktar manage to be on time today?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central region chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poynton and Jason Hodnett go head to head cooking their main courses for the veteran judge. With Mark and Aktar fighting for the lead can Jason sneak under the radar to take them by surprise?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Central chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poyton and Jason Hodnett are all striving for one of two spots in the regional final. Will Jasons elaborate chocolate medal dessert see him triumph or sent home empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central region chefs cook all four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge Max Hastings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course and as the chefs arrive they discover theres added competition following the London and South East regions shock result. The judges are joined by D-Day veteran George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by Ken Sturdy who landed on Sword Beach on D-Day and who helped judge the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the half-way point in finals week and those chefs who have yet to get a dish onto the shortlist are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by Baroness Trumpington who worked as a cypher clerk at Bletchley Park from 1941 to 1946.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the chefs to get their dish in contention for the final banquet and those whove failed to finish in the top three so far are going all out. Joining the judges is Celia Sandys, Winston Churchills granddaughter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the moment of truth as the victorious four must put their finest hours on a plate for those who served both on the front line and the home front, such as D-Day veterans Ken Sturdy and George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands top chefs, Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee, and Graham Campbell - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge. Having missed out last year, returning finalist Jak is determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell are battling to plate up a perfect main course for this weeks veteran chef. Self-taught Jimmy is honouring his mum with his rendition of her Chinese beef stew.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today its the Scottish chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow, and with one chef in the lead after a triumphant main, the pressures on for the other two to plate up perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Highway to Heaven

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters

programme.sub-title: A Family Torn Apart By A Haunted House

programme.title: Haunted Hospitals

programme.title: Haunted Hospitals
programme.desc: A look at spirits intent on punishing the living, featuring a nighttime intruder at a nursing home, a vengeful ghost who appears before a nurses aide, and an aggressive entity that targets a switchboard operator.

programme.title: Egypt: New Discoveries, Ancient Mysteries
programme.desc: The truth about the pharaoh who amended Egyptian religion to monotheism.

programme.title: Egypt: New Discoveries, Ancient Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Heliopolis: The Cult Of The Sun
programme.desc: In Heliopolis to uncover the importance of ancient Egypts Cult of the Sun.

programme.title: Egypt: New Discoveries, Ancient Mysteries
programme.desc: Featuring details and information about the excavation of Tutankamon.

programme.title: Ramesses The Great - Legendary Pharoah of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Ramesses the Great, born in the 14th century BC, was part of Egypts prosperous Bronze Age era.

programme.title: The Rise of Napoleon Bonaparte A Military Prodigy
programme.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte, born in 1769 in Corsica, came from minor nobility and claimed Roman heritage.

programme.title: 1805: The Battle of Austerlitz
programme.desc: Recounting an important day in December of 1805, which became one of the most decisive battles and the greatest victory of the Napoleonic era.

programme.title: The Endurance: Shackletons Legendary Antarctic Expedition
programme.desc: Explorer Ernest Shackleton and his team become stranded for nearly two years in severe antarctic conditions. Narrated by Liam Neeson.

programme.title: Secrets and Mysteries of Christopher Columbus
programme.desc: Investigating the true origins of Christopher Columbus through historical records and testimony from experts and Columbus living relatives.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher discovers what life was really like for ordinary Egyptians 3,500 years ago, during the height of the incredible civilisation of ancient Egypt. Joann explores how people lived during this time, from the tightly packed houses and villages of the common people, to the clothes they wore and the food they ate.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher reveals a strange and mysterious world: the ancient Egyptian afterlife. To them waking life was just a dress rehearsal. Joann clambers into rarely visited tombs, explores a treasure trove of long-buried objects and examines spectacular mummies to discover just why the Egyptians spent a fortune preparing for death.

programme.title: Egypt: New Discoveries, Ancient Mysteries
programme.desc: Tour the basement rooms of the Cairo Museum to find many forgotten Egyptian treasures, sarcophagi, and mummies buried under decades of dust.

programme.title: Egypt: New Discoveries, Ancient Mysteries
programme.desc: Journey through a maze of private tombs buried in the foothills of Egypt and discover the Pharaohs.

programme.title: Egypt: New Discoveries, Ancient Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Giza: The Lost City Of Pyramid Builders
programme.desc: Explore the origins of Gizas great pyramids.

programme.title: Ancient Giants: Legends of Anakims and Nephilim
programme.desc: Anthropologists may have discovered an ancient 7-14 foot tall giant human subgroup.

programme.title: Henry V - Agincourt and The Hundred Years War
programme.desc: Henry V of England, born 1386, led during power struggles and the Hundred Years War.

programme.title: Quest for the Lost Tribes
programme.desc: Filmmaker Simcha Jacobovic searches the globe for answers and investigates the lost tribes of Israel. From 12 mentioned in the Bible only Judah and Benjamin remain after they survived the Assyrian purge of nearly three millennia ago.
Following the days of the last World War, with a focus on Allies strategy tensions.

In 1945 the victorious Australian men began returning from the war in Europe and the South Pacific. The women, excited and relieved to see their men return, have little knowledge of the demons that have followed them home.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Finds include the lost WWI training trenches on Salisbury Plain, Britains first `double henge, discovered just down the road from Stonehenge, where the evidence suggests our ancestors feasted and made sacred offerings as part of a visit to the ritualistic Stonehenge landscape, and luxury foreign goods discovered at Tintagel, the legendary childhood home of King Arthur.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Mafia and Britain
programme.desc: Ross Kemp investigates and exposes Britains surprising and deep-rooted links with the mafia, dating as far back as the 1920s.

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: Using homemade instruments to carve a hole in the roof after climbing prison pipes, eight inmates escaped from a New Mexico detention centre in 2008.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of Cardiff castle over more than 2,000 years and reveals how it became transformed from a strategically important Roman and Norman stronghold to a lavish fairy-tale Victorian home.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: In medieval England, the North was ruled from York. Dan Jones explores the history of its castle, from the Viking Jorvik and the Normans, through the dark days of the anti-Jewish pogrom and the imprisonment of the Knights Templar, to one of its most colourful inmates - the legendary highwayman Dick Turpin.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Edwardian Britain by visiting Cardiff, which grew rich selling the coal that powered the Empire. Alice hears the stories of the Edwardian miners and their backbreaking and often dangerous work.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the English Civil War through the history of Oxford, the city at the heart of a battle between an autocratic king and his rebellious parliament.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comedian Loyiso Gola embark on a road trip through South Africa to explore the UKs imperial history in the country, from colonial wars to the deals that eventually led to the development of apartheid.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comic Kevin Kropinyeri reveal that Captain James Cook was not really a captain, explore the Aboriginal history of Australian cricket, and uncover the often brutal land grabs that impacted Indigenous Australians.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team is in the north of Britain where they uncover evidence of the first Roman siege in Britain, Scottish man-made islands and a famous monastery.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Finds include: revelations from `Britains Pompeii, the 3,000-year-old perfectly preserved village in Cambridgeshire, including how Bronze Age ancestors designed their homes, and their kitchens packed with food and equipment, and the theatre where Shakespeare premiered Romeo and Juliet and Henry V.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: In medieval England, the North was ruled from York. Dan Jones explores the history of its castle, from the Viking Jorvik and the Normans, through the dark days of the anti-Jewish pogrom and the imprisonment of the Knights Templar, to one of its most colourful inmates - the legendary highwayman Dick Turpin.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon familys manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores Manchesters history of protest and revolution during the Industrial Revolution, from Marx to the Suffragettes and the Peterloo massacre.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Roman Britain by studying the history of Chester, Britains most Roman town.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tonys walk takes him back to 1940 when the sleepy county of Dorset became the frontline in the war against Hitler. His five-day, 60-mile walk along the Jurassic coast reveals the countys hidden World War II story. Starting by the defences on Chesil Beach, Tonys journey encompasses stunning scenery and amazing acts of ingenuity and bravery as he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britains beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the worlds industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2019s most exciting archaeological finds.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the North, the remains of a Tudor house in Leicestershire were thought to be the childhood home of Englands forgotten Queen, Lady Jane Grey. But when archaeologists excavate, they find more than they bargained for.

programme.title: Britains Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: A host of top celebrities explore uniquely British preoccupations and passions in an attempt to find out what makes the British tick.

programme.title: Britains Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: Suggs discovers the history of the British love of the humble hostelry and explores a load of interesting issues along the way, from womens role in brewing, and the community role of the pub, to the pub as a place of literary and musical creativity.

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: The Freemasons have been meeting since the 14th century. From an ancient stoneworkers guild through to the modern era, their mythologies, beliefs, and secret rituals have inspired countless works of fiction and speculation.

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.sub-title: The Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn
programme.desc: A society that included Bram Stoker, Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, and H P Lovecraft as a members. The Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn was a secret society that dabbled in the occult, laying the groundwork for modern Wiccanism and esoteric practices.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads off for a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire to tell the story of life and death in the last centuries of the Stone Age. His route takes him through the greatest concentration of prehistoric sites in Europe.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. Tony Robinson tracks their 50-mile walk from Penrith past Ullswater to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team is in the north of Britain where they uncover evidence of the first Roman siege in Britain, Scottish man-made islands and a famous monastery.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: When an environmental health officer disappears and a dismembered arm is washed up on the shore of the Humber Estuary, Frost is called in to investigate. Suspecting foul play he links the murder to a meat factory the health officer had been looking into. Frost also has troubles on the home front with the arrival of his new lodger and his uninvited dog.

The Safari Rally was first held in 1953 as the East African Coronation Safari in Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika and a celebration of the coronation of Queen Elizabeth II.

Given their history, hard-up Milo is anticipating an easy pay day, but Nicoles dogged determination to get her story drags them both into a world of trouble.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: This episode looks at three daughters and their love for their mother. Eighty-year-old Rose has fluid on her lungs and is having trouble breathing. Her three daughters Christine, Sandra and Debbie put on brave faces around their mothers bedside, convincing her that everything will be all right, but in the relatives room there is high emotion as they tearfully contemplate life without her.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Quirky couple Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome hope to impress with their two-star paradise Eden Lodge in Falmouth, Cornwall. However, with two of the guests - Charlotte Churchs parents James and Maria Church - already saying that they wouldnt stay anywhere below a four star, it could be an interesting day. From the offset the guests are wildly unimpressed by their surroundings, from peeling wallpaper to a dirty toilet brush.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The boys are in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul and Michelle want to retire to Spains beautiful Costa Cálida, famous for its year-round warmth and relaxing atmosphere. Jonnie Irwin shows them what their £60,000 budget can buy.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This weeks competition is in and around Liverpool, where the first to host is 41-year-old taxi business owner Rob, who plans a Scouse-themed evening.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its night two in Liverpool, and the turn of businesswoman Sam to try and impress her guests with her South-African-themed evening, but her food is a bit hit and miss.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third night in Liverpool, gym worker Toria plans to scoop the grand because losing is something that doesnt come easily to her.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Liverpools fourth host this week is undergraduate Inzy, who is on a mission to prove that students can cook as he eyes the prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night of the competition in and around Liverpool, property developer Christine plans to wow her guests with a Spanish holiday themed dinner party.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week begins at the Old Rose and Crown in Birmingham, with hosts Andrew and Jo Hodges. But problems with paintwork and missing milk leave them with some meaty feedback to chew over.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jill and Inez host the second visit of the week, at Middlewick in Glastonbury. The guests are confounded by a cottage next to a camping pod, and the avocado breakfast offering.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Griffin Inn in Lincolnshire, hosts Jamie and Leila take the guests on a tour of their family heritage. Guest Jo dishes the dirt on Jamies decorating, and damp patches are a problem.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Crossroads House in Carlisle, owned by Julie and Alistair. The guests note the BandBs roadside location and mismatched decor, and Julie lacks confidence in the kitchen.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its feedback time as the BandBers discuss paying for breakfast and picky comments about paintwork. Harsh truths are aired before the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: This time Tim and Fuzz are in unknown territory as they take on 28-year-old Charlies Lexus IS200 and transform it into a full-blown drift car.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw seek out classic cars with deserving owners. Using all their guile and restoration knowledge they sneak away clapped out classics without their owners knowing then and get the much loved motors back on the road.

programme.title: The Dog House Australia
programme.desc: Three-legged Mr Norman has the cuteness, but he needs to tick all of the Peacocks boxes. And the already-crowded Sorrell house needs to make room for Turbo Tess.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Geoffs selling up in Spain to build a flood-resistant architectural marvel on the Essex coast. After the foundations are drilled into the ground, Geoff hits double trouble, the Covid-19 pandemic and a collapse in the Spanish property market.

programme.title: Saving Country Houses with Penelope Keith
programme.desc: As the summer ends, rain and storms threaten Mappertons annual plant fair. Chavenages sauna opens. And the lakes at Whitmore become a tourist hotspot.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The series continues with a powerful cautionary episode about events that shock people into reflecting on the way they live their lives. Neil was driving a friends sports car when he was involved in a head-on collision with another vehicle. After remaining trapped in the wreckage for over an hour, hes flown by helicopter to Kings AandE. Meanwhile in Minors, two patients have come in as the result of daredevil antics.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The RTS award-winning series continues with an episode about romance and the complexity of relationships. Its Valentines Day, and at Kings College Hospitals Accident and Emergency Department love is in the air.

programme.desc: Alvin sneaks into Simons new virtual reality game and is transported to a world in need of a hero; the chipmunks discover Officer Dangus has a twin brother thats a bully and encourage Dangus to stand up to him.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin plays a trick on Theodores pancakes. Alvin wants to take over the drum kit.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When an accident forces Simon to wear his old glasses, he becomes the target of the school bully. So Simon builds a homemade robot bodyguard!

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Dave comes down with a bad cold, Alvin quickly offers to take over Dad duty. But he quickly learns that parenting is a lot harder than he ever imagined.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittany is preparing a class speech on inner beauty when she gets a rash on her face. Can she practice what she preaches? Alvin thinks that Kevin has a secret crush on one of the Chipettes. When the girls find out, they try to let Kevin down gently.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Oh Brother Where Art Thou; The Wall
programme.desc: Alvin thinks hes losing his mind when Simon goes missing and no one else even remembers who Simon is. When the girls at school cast votes for the cutest chipmunk on the bathroom wall, Alvin worries about the results.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Mysterions; The Crow That Ate My Homework
programme.desc: When Theodore discovers Alvin and Simon have a secret club, he promises not to tell anyone if they let him join. Dave promises to take Alvin to a water park if he gets a good grade on his test, but then a crow steals it.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin gets a lesson in parenting after he kidnaps Simons robot; when Dave takes away Alvins phone, the boredom Alvin experiences is enough to drive him crazy.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: A single father experiences the trials and tribulations of raising a group of young chipmunks who are also famous rock stars touring the world with their best friends, the Chipettes.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin is paired with Simon to work on a science project, he accidentally creates a potion that turns Theodore into a giant. When all the childrens phones go missing at school, Alvin is blamed for the theft and sets out to clear his name.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When the boys accidentally break Daves new flat screen TV. they decide to become fugitives on the lam. Jeanette and Eleanor act as Sherlock Holmes and Watson when Theodores teddy bear mysteriously disappears.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin talks Dave into buying a used tour bus, Dave has no idea of the problems ahead. Alvin signs the Chipmunks up for a game show that forces losing contestants to perform dangerous stunts.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvins attempts to earn money as an Internet influencer almost puts a local toy store out of business; the Chipmunks accidentally ruin Daves new pavilion, and they allow Miss Miller to take the fall.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Run Aimee Run; Theo vs. Simon
programme.desc: The Munks help a world-famous runner design a pair of prosthetic legs for a race shes set to run in. Theodore embarks on a quest to find one thing he can do better than Simon, and he quickly realises it wont be easy.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittany takes up painting to impress her fashion idol, but her idol only likes her art after Alvin ruins it; Alvin is convinced that Julie and her son are trying to move in to their home, so he tries to stop the invasion.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After learning about the Knights of the Round Table, Theodore wants to be a knight and struggles to do good deeds. Alvin and Simon create a fake snake to help Dave get over his fear of snakes.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin thinks Theodores teddy bear can tell the future, Alvin goes all out to make Talking Teddy his friend. Alvin lies to Theodore about a fake holiday to get out of chores, so Dave forces Alvin to make the holiday real.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Fed up with seeing how dependent The Chipmunks have become on technology, Dave takes them camping for a weekend, without electricity, cell service or the Internet.

programme.title: America the Bountiful
programme.desc: How seeds are saved; a garden of living history that captures pre and post contact plants in Arizona; making blue corn cakes.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded
programme.desc: Investigators trace the rise and fall of a thriving metropolis in North America known as Cahokia. Bigger than London, Rome and Paris, the mighty civilisation exploded into existence in 900 AD but vanished less than 400 years later.

programme.title: Berlin: Stories of the City
programme.desc: Berlin has always welcomed outsiders. But it has also been a battleground invaded by the French, taken over by the Nazis, occupied by the Russians and then divided down the middle.

programme.title: The War
programme.desc: With U-Boats menacing the East Coast, and little good news from overseas, many worry that the United States is utterly unprepared to fight in a `total war but after six months of gruelling combat on Guadalcanal, the Americans finally stop Japans expansion in the Pacific.

programme.title: War Machine: World War II
programme.desc: The evolution of naval warfare sees a shift from battleships to submarines and aircraft carriers. Control of supply lines shapes the war at sea, as decisive battles in WWII become increasingly dependant on the mass production of large-scale vessels.

programme.title: Hall and Oates - Live at The Sydney Entertainment Centre
programme.desc: Daryl Hall and John Oates wisely reunited a few years ago after a string of solo projects, and they put on the kind of show that makes it hard to believe they even considered going their separate ways. Here they are at the Sydney Entertainment Centre.

programme.title: Depeche Mode - Rock Am Ring

programme.title: Britney Spears - Live from London
programme.desc: Filmed at Roundhouse, London, this concert film will give all fans a spectacular Live experience from start to finish. Britney Spears performs her hits including Baby One More Time, Oops! I Did It Again, Toxic and many more.

programme.title: Lana Del Rey - Live in London 2012

programme.desc: The Lumineers bring energetic American folk-rock to the picturesque Musilac Festival. One will not be able to resist clapping along and stomping their feet to the bands raw sound and enchanting music.

programme.desc: Ed Sheeran makes a large venue feel intimate; with his guitar and vocal skills, Sheeran delivers a strong performance in 2014 at the Roundhouse Art Center in London.

Whilst searching for her birth mother, Sian Jones discovered she has a sister out there somewhere and Long Lost Family help her search for both mother and sister. Meanwhile, Teleena Faux always believed she was the youngest of six siblings until she found evidence of a younger brother. Teleena has been desperate to find him ever since.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Foundlings are people who were abandoned as babies, often in the first hours and days of their lives. Born without a trace, they have had no way to unlock the secrets of their past. By combining new DNA technology with painstaking detective work, the mysteries of people with unknown beginnings who are desperate for answers can now be solved. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the team behind `Long Lost Family use an extraordinary new technique to try and identify birth families and find answers for people who have spent decades trying to find out where they came from.

programme.desc: A chronicle of Palestines fight for independence in 1947 and beyond. The story focuses on a moderate Israeli resistance leader and embraces his love for a non-Jewish army nurse, his differences with the extremist Irgun factions and his struggle to emancipate hundreds of survivors of Nazi concentration camps trapped on the Exodus, a vessel blockaded in a Cypriot harbour by British warships.

programme.title: Twister

They have to get to the tornado first to be able to test a radical new device, by inserting it directly into the heart of the storm.

programme.title: Love Hurts
programme.desc: Marvin is a Milwaukee realtor who receives a crimson envelope from a former partner-in-crime whom he left for dead. He now finds himself thrust back into a world of ruthless hit men and double-crosses that turn his open houses into deadly war zones.

programme.title: Bad Boys: Ride or Die
programme.desc: When their late police captain gets linked to drug cartels, wisecracking Miami cops Mike Lowrey and Marcus Burnett embark on a dangerous mission to clear his name.

programme.title: Terminator Salvation

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga

programme.title: Fast and Furious 5

programme.title: Fast and Furious 6
programme.desc: Since Dom and Brians heist in Rio they must live as fugitives. Meanwhile, Agent Hobbs has been tracking a gang of lethally skilled mercenary drivers. Hobbs asks Dom and his crew for help in exchange for full pardons for everyone.

programme.title: Troy
programme.desc: When Trojan prince Paris woos Helen, Queen of Sparta, away from her husband Menelaus, the legendary warrior Achilles is called to lead the armies of Greece against the city fortress of Troy.

programme.title: Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom
programme.desc: Three years after the destruction of the Jurassic World theme park, Owen Grady and Claire Dearing return to the island of Isla Nublar to save the remaining dinosaurs from a volcano thats about to erupt. They soon encounter terrifying new breeds of gigantic dinosaurs, while uncovering a conspiracy that threatens the entire planet.

programme.title: Enter the Dragon
programme.desc: A kung fu expert is assigned to penetrate an island fortress to destroy an opium and white slavery empire.

programme.title: Fast and Furious Presents: Hobbs and Shaw
programme.desc: Brixton Lorr is a cybernetically enhanced soldier who possesses superhuman strength, a brilliant mind and a lethal pathogen that could wipe out half of the worlds population. Its now up to hulking lawman Luke Hobbs and lawless operative Deckard Shaw to put aside their past differences and work together to prevent the seemingly indestructible Lorr from destroying humanity.

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
programme.desc: Epic tale in which an intrepid archaeologist tries to beat a band of Nazis to a unique religious relic which is central to their plans for world domination. Battling against a snake phobia and a vengeful ex-girlfriend, Indiana Jones is in constant peril, making hairs-breadth escapes at every turn in this celebration of the innocent adventure movies of an earlier era.

programme.title: DC Super Hero Girls: Legends of Atlantis
programme.desc: Siren and Mera steal the powerful Book of Legends. The Super Hero Girls head to Atlantis to recover the book before Siren can use it to create an army and take over the entire ocean.

programme.title: Tim Burtons Corpse Bride
programme.desc: Anxious not to make a mistake in his wedding ceremony, Victor goes to the woods to practise his vows. Unfortunately, he slides the ring onto the twig-finger of a long-dead bride who has been waiting years for a suitor.

programme.title: Small Foot
programme.desc: Migo is a friendly Yeti who discovers something that he didnt know existed: a human. When his fellow villagers refuse to believe him, Migo embarks on an epic journey to find the mysterious creature that can put him back in good graces.

programme.title: Smurfs: The Lost Village
programme.desc: With the evil wizard Gargamel hot on their trail, Smurfette, Brainy, Clumsy and Hefty embark on a journey through the Forbidden Forest to find a mysterious village.

programme.title: Rango
programme.desc: An ordinary chameleon, Rango, winds up in the town of Dirt, a lawless outpost in the Wild West in need of a new sheriff.

programme.title: The LEGO NINJAGO Movie
programme.desc: Master Wu, as wise-cracking as he is wise, leads a fierce team of secret ninja warriors to defeat the evil warlord Garmadon and save Ninjago City.

programme.desc: A life-saving surgery fuses together a police officer and his faithful police dog -- and Dog Man is born. Learning to embrace his new identity, Dog Man must stop feline supervillain Petey the Cat from cloning himself and going on a crime spree.

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit
programme.desc: The village is being overrun by rabbits and Wallace has a plan to alter their minds so that they will leave the vegetable allotments alone. However, his plan goes awry and the rabbit problem escalates when the villagers discover a monstrous `were-rabbit destroying everything in its sights. Will Wallace and Gromit be able to solve the conundrum before the creature gets shot?

programme.title: The LEGO Movie 2: The Second Part
programme.desc: The citizens of Bricksburg face a dangerous new threat when LEGO DUPLO invaders from outer space start to wreck everything in their path. The battle to defeat the enemy and restore harmony to the LEGO universe takes Emmet, Lucy, Batman and the rest of their friends to faraway, unexplored worlds that test their courage and creativity.

programme.title: Batman Unlimited: Animal Instincts
programme.desc: When the beast-themed Animilitia plague Gotham, Batman swings out to investigate. But with The Penguin and his cyber-animals added to the mix, Batman will need the help of Green Arrow and The Flash to bring them to heel.

programme.desc: Siren and Mera steal the powerful Book of Legends. The Super Hero Girls head to Atlantis to recover the book before Siren can use it to create an army and take over the entire ocean.

programme.title: The Ballad of Wallis Island
programme.desc: Old tensions resurface when former bandmates who were former lovers reunite for a private show at the island home of an eccentric millionaire.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Bingeable

programme.desc: Disgraced FBI agents Marcus and Kevin go to bizarre lengths to catch a devious millionaire. They disguise themselves as dumb white socialites Brittany and Tiffany Wilson, the heiresses to a hotel fortune who are under threat. Gross-out comedic farce from the makers of `Scary Movie.

programme.title: American Pie: The Wedding
programme.desc: Jim and Michelle are getting married and Jim struggles to win over his future in-laws and deal with his embarrassing dad. Meanwhile, Stifler isnt invited to the ceremony, but hes determined not to be left out.

programme.title: The Hangover
programme.desc: A crazy Las Vegas bachelor party turns into a race against time when three hung-over groomsmen awaken after a night of drunken debauchery to find that the groom has gone missing, and attempt to get him to the alter in time for his wedding.

programme.title: The Hangover Part II
programme.desc: A group of friends go to Thailand for a quiet and restrained stag party, but are dismayed to wake up in a mysterious hotel room, with a man missing and no memory of the night before. Of course, Alan manages to get himself in with the crowd.

programme.title: The Hangover Part III
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Doug take Alan on a road trip to help him through a personal crisis.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop II
programme.desc: The hard-nosed Detroit cop from the original film returns to Los Angeles to help solve another case. This time he must direct his efforts towards unraveling the Alphabet Crimes, a series of robberies committed by leather-jacketed punks. The investigation puts him on the trail of an illegal arms dealer and his hit lady.

programme.title: This Is Spinal Tap
programme.desc: Satirical `rockumentary following ageing British heavy metal band Spinal Tap - David St Hubbins, Nigel Tufnel, Derek Smalls and their latest hapless drummer - on the comeback trail, promoting their album `Smell the Glove on a US tour. The film features many moments that have passed into legend, including exploding drummers and amplifiers that go up to 11.

programme.title: American Pie: Reunion
programme.desc: Outrageous antics ensue when Jim, Michelle, Stifler and the gang get back together for their 13-year high school reunion.

programme.title: Jackass: The Movie
programme.desc: Johnny Knoxville and his mad companions leap onto the big screen. Expect the usual mad gross-out japes, from making snow cones out of urine to using the display toilets in bathroom outlets, and from racing golf carts over hallowed greens to tightrope walking over alligators.

programme.title: Jackass 3
programme.desc: Johnny Knoxville and the gang unleash their most insane stunts yet, from roller-skating with a buffalo herd, to playing tennis with a beehive.

programme.title: Ted 2

programme.title: Coach Carter
programme.desc: Based on the true story of Coach Ken Carter, a controversial high school basketball coach who received national attention when he benched his undefeated team due to their collective poor academic record in 1999. In the face of much criticism, Carter fought to keep his methods, determined to show the boys that they need to rely on more than sports for their futures.

programme.title: I, Tonya
programme.desc: In 1991, talented figure skater Tonya Harding becomes the first American woman to complete a triple axel during a competition. In 1994, her world comes crashing down when her ex-husband conspires to injure Nancy Kerrigan, a fellow Olympic hopeful, in a poorly conceived attack that forces the young woman to withdraw from the national championship. Hardings life and legacy instantly become tarnished as shes forever associated with one of the most infamous scandals in sports history.

programme.title: Escape to Victory
programme.desc: When ex-German international and camp commandant Van Steiner suggests a match between the POWs and the Germans, it swiftly escalates into a propaganda exercise.

programme.desc: Struggling with his dual identity, failed comedian Arthur Fleck stumbles upon true love while incarcerated at Arkham State Hospital. Awaiting trial for the murder of five people, Fleck soon discovers the music thats always been inside him.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Escape to Victory
programme.desc: When ex-German international and camp commandant Van Steiner suggests a match between the POWs and the Germans, it swiftly escalates into a propaganda exercise.

programme.title: Turtles All the Way Down

programme.title: Pokemon Detective Pikachu
programme.desc: A young man joins forces with Detective Pikachu to unravel the mystery behind his fathers disappearance. Chasing clues through the streets of Ryme City, the dynamic duo soon discover a devious plot that poses a threat to the Pokemon universe.

programme.title: Paulie
programme.desc: A wisecracking parrot caged in a research lab tells his life story to a lonely janitor. Before coming to the science lab, he has passed through the hands of a succession of owners, from a petty criminal to a man who runs a parrot-dancing act. Touched by his colourful story, the janitor conspires to liberate the talking bird.

programme.title: The Wiz
programme.desc: In this version of the classic story - based on the smash Broadway musical - Dorothy is a shy 24-year-old Harlem kindergarten teacher who is brought by a snowstorm to the mysterious Land of Oz, a sprawling, decaying, nightmarish, and dangerous megalopolis that resembles the physical characteristics of New York City.

programme.title: The Karate Kid
programme.desc: A new kid at a school makes the mistake of dating the ex-girlfriend of the school bully, whose gang then make his life a misery. He then bumps into a handyman who also happens to be a karate master. The handyman teaches him martial arts and he eventually meets up with his tormentors at a tournament, but will they fight fair?

programme.title: Lemony Snickets A Series of Unfortunate Events
programme.desc: Three orphans are sent to live with their uncle Count Olaf, but he only wants their family fortune. What follows is a battle of wits.

programme.title: Scary Girl
programme.desc: To save her father from a dangerous scientist in a world shrouded in darkness, and prevent her planets destruction, a young girl overcomes her fears to travel to a mysterious city of light.

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
programme.desc: The sequel to `Madagascar, in which New York Zoo animals, Alex the Lion, Marty the Zebra, Melman the Giraffe and Gloria the Hippo, still stranded on Madagascar, start to leave the island. All of a sudden, they land in the wilderness of Africa, where Alex meets the rest of his family, but has trouble communicating with them after spending so much time at the Central Park Zoo.

programme.title: The Emoji Movie
programme.desc: Three emojis embark on an epic adventure through a smartphone to save their world from being deleted.

programme.title: Bee Movie
programme.desc: Barry B Benson is an everybee with a dream. He escapes the hive and makes contact with a human, the florist Vanessa Bloome. Enraged to discover that humans have been stealing their honey goodness, Barry sets out on a crusade that will change the ecosystem forever. Will it be for the better? A DreamWorks animation.

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz
programme.desc: Perennial family favourite about Dorothy, a young girl from the American Midwest, who is swept by a cyclone to a magical kingdom over the rainbow. There she meets a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who join her on a quest to find the all-powerful Wizard of Oz, the only man who can help her get back to to Kansas. However, the Wicked Witch of the West has other plans for Dorothy and her new friends.

programme.title: The Karate Kid Part II
programme.desc: Old rivalries and new adversaries await Daniel and Miyagi as they travel to Okinawa to visit Miyagis ailing father.

programme.title: The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey
programme.desc: At the urging of the wizard Gandalf, unassuming hobbit Bilbo Baggins joins 13 dwarves on a perilous quest to reclaim the kingdom of Erebor and gains possession of the powerful One Ring.

programme.title: Paulie
programme.desc: A wisecracking parrot caged in a research lab tells his life story to a lonely janitor. Before coming to the science lab, he has passed through the hands of a succession of owners, from a petty criminal to a man who runs a parrot-dancing act. Touched by his colourful story, the janitor conspires to liberate the talking bird.

programme.title: Ride On
programme.desc: Washed-up stuntman Luo seeks help from his estranged daughter and her lawyer boyfriend when his beloved stunt horse may be auctioned off to pay his debts. Luo and Red Hare become a media sensation when their fight with debt collectors goes viral.

programme.desc: Washed-up stuntman Luo seeks help from his estranged daughter and her lawyer boyfriend when his beloved stunt horse may be auctioned off to pay his debts. Luo and Red Hare become a media sensation when their fight with debt collectors goes viral.

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview
programme.desc: Previews of the great films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights showing on Sky.

programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil
programme.desc: Lighthearted adventures of a pair of news vendors in New York - one deaf, one blind - who go on the run when they are blamed for the murder of a customer. Each has seen - or heard - just a whiff of a clue, and a frantic chase ensues as the duo struggle to clear their names.

programme.title: Billy Madison
programme.desc: Billy is the son of hotel chain magnate Brian Madison and expects to inherit the family fortune, but hes shocked when his father stipulates he wont unless he passes 12th grade in just 24 weeks. It will be tough, since he never passed 1st grade. Determined to prove his father wrong, Billy enrols at school.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: An alcoholic millionaire is threatened with losing his inheritance if he does not marry the girl of his parents choosing. Despite his dislike for his prospective bride, he bows to the inevitable only to meet a light-fingered waitress who steals his heart. John Gielgud won an Oscar for his role as Arthurs loyal but outspoken valet.

programme.title: Singin in the Rain
programme.desc: When the transition is being made from silent films to `talkies, everyone has trouble adapting. Don and Lina have been cast repeatedly as a romantic couple, but when their latest film is remade into a musical, only Don has the voice for the new singing part. After a lot of practise with a diction coach, Lina still sounds terrible, and Kathy, a bright young aspiring actress, is hired to record over her voice.

programme.title: Sleepless in Seattle
programme.desc: A recently engaged woman becomes obsessed with meeting the grieving widower she heard on a late night radio show. Sam, the widower, receives hundreds of letters following the radio phone-in which was instigated by his son Jonah.

programme.title: Youve Got Mail
programme.desc: Two rival bookstore owners are unaware that they are conducting a secret romance with each other on the Internet. When they discover the truth, they must prevent their business rivalry from interfering with their growing personal intimacy.

programme.title: Big Daddy
programme.desc: A thirtysomething slacker attempts to inject some stability into his life by adopting an impressionable five-year-old.

programme.title: The Wedding Singer
programme.desc: When one-time rock hopeful Robbie is jilted at the altar, he vents his bitterness in song, even when singing at other peoples weddings. Only true love can redeem the broken hearted songster but fortunately, hes about to meet a woman who will spin him right round (like a record).

programme.title: Police Academy
programme.desc: Misfits realise their dreams of becoming cops when a mayoral decree forces the police academy to open its doors to all.

programme.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment
programme.desc: Mahoney and friends have graduated from the Police Academy and are issued with their first assignments. As before, their cheerfulness and devotion to duty outweighs any shortcomings, but are they ready to do battle with a band of graffiti daubing terrorists?

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training
programme.desc: Slapstick farce with the incompetent cops as they set about saving their beloved academy from closure. Cutbacks mean that only one of the two police training schools can stay open, so Commandant Lassard appeals to the motley crew of graduates to come and train new recruits. However, the rival academy intends to sabotage their plans by cutting their training time down to a minimum.

programme.title: Pulp Fiction
programme.desc: A hit man falls for his bosss wife when he is asked to escort her on a date, a boxer reneges on his promise to throw a big fight, and a couple attempt a heist that rapidly escalates out of control.

programme.title: Sleepers
programme.desc: Eleven years after their horrific experiences at reform school, Tommy Marcano and John Reilly have become two-bit American gangsters, Lorenzo Carcaterra is a reporter and Michael Sullivan an assistant DA. The four are reunited when they chance across one of the abusive guards and take the chance for revenge.

programme.title: Sleepers
programme.desc: Eleven years after their horrific experiences at reform school, Tommy Marcano and John Reilly have become two-bit American gangsters, Lorenzo Carcaterra is a reporter and Michael Sullivan an assistant DA. The four are reunited when they chance across one of the abusive guards and take the chance for revenge.

programme.title: Tootsie
programme.desc: An out-of-work actor dresses in drag for an audition, only to land the female lead in a TV soap. When he becomes the most popular character on the show, he feels compelled to carry the persona into his everyday life. While bringing him phenomenal success as an `actress, life as a woman also has its problems - especially when he falls for an actress with whom he works.

programme.title: Bye Bye Birdie
programme.desc: Based on events surrounding Elvis Presleys experience of the US Army draft, this film focuses on fictional pop star Conrad Birdie, who is called up for National Service, preventing him from starring in the movie that was to make his agent rich. programme.desc: Clara and her daughter Clarita are chosen for release back into the wild and set off on a dangerous journey to the protected wilderness. But a flash flood threatens their convoy.

programme.title: Rooted
programme.desc: In the Afromontane forests of the Soutpansberg grows a giant Outeniqua Yellowwood. This is the story of how this colossal tree hosts, feeds and shelters an array of plants and animals in the bows of her branches.

programme.title: Land of Gremlins
programme.desc: A family of lemurs in the rainforest of Mantadia National Park.

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: The plains and woodlands of the Luangwa Valley are the wildest and most merciless of all neighbourhoods where duels between predator and prey are played out both day and night. For the animals that call this home, its a daily battle to stay alive.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans and apes at Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre.

programme.title: Americas Wild Seasons
programme.desc: Summer is the time of plenty across America. Its a chance for animals to reap natures bounty while they can. The years young begin to explore their world, while new parents make use of the long days to make sure they grow up strong.

programme.title: Islands
programme.desc: Hyenas claim the largest island in the delta, but must defend it. Elephants and hippos sculpt the waterways and make new homes for smaller creatures.

programme.title: Into the Wild Colombia
programme.desc: From Colombias high plains of the Páramo to its tropical forests, hummingbirds have evolved into an extraordinary number of species. Whether battling freezing rain or jungle predators, each mother must struggle to build a nest, protect her eggs.

programme.title: Ol Pejeta Diaries
programme.desc: Four North African White Rhinos are settled at Ol Pejeta. The staff must prepare them for a return, not just to life in a semi-wild environment, but also to try and trigger their wild instincts, particularly their desire to breed.

programme.title: Ol Pejeta Diaries
programme.desc: The Black Rhino is the most endangered species in the world and when they start dying in rapid success, Ol Pejeta brings their expertise to help figure out whats killing them-before its too late.

programme.desc: Ahead of the Tyne-Wear Derby, we catch up with former Newcastle striker Demba Ba at USL Dunkerque, where he is Head of Football Operations. He opens up about the challenges and rewards of his new role, while reflecting on a remarkable career in the Premier League that took him to West Ham United, Newcastle United and Chelsea. programme.desc: Ahead of the Tyne-Wear Derby, we catch up with former Newcastle striker Demba Ba at USL Dunkerque, where he is Head of Football Operations. programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: It is February and the team approach the half way point in their year on the Edwardian Farm. To mark the occasion this episode explores one single day in a typical Edwardian farmers life. Incorporating a remarkable cache of letters written in the 1900s in a cottage at Morwhellham Quay, `A Day in the Life reveals the hidden stories of how ordinary rural Edwardians got by.

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: Marcus makes plans to expand the smallholdings herb patch. A trip to Gloucestershire to see the UKs largest herb collection shows that even someone as experienced as Marcus can still learn a lot about ingredients.

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: It is March and Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn greet the long-awaited arrival of spring. It is time to bring in the daffodil harvest. During its heyday in the early 20th century, the Tamar Valley was the largest producer of early daffodils in Britain - the result of the regions mild climate combined with the arrival of a railway, which meant produce could be delivered to towns and cities across Britain within hours of bei.

programme.title: Country House Auction
programme.desc: Castle Durrow sets the stage, as four unique antiques have their moment under the hammer. A Fender guitar signed by the Rolling Stones piques the interest of dealer Ian. And a 400 year-old Tibetan dagger made from a meteorite prompts a bidding war.

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: With a rotting door and botched fuse box, Jo and Pauls kitchen in their new cottage needs some serious work. Dave Wellman is the man for the job.

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: With rotten joists, woodworm infestation, broken brickwork and dodgy plumbing, Dave has his work cut out at Simon and Lorraines new home.

programme.title: Supermarket Shopping Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg and Babita look at one of the biggest battlegrounds, health. Gregg discovers the exotic ingredient being used to produce a healthier burger and goes on the trail of a major health trend, spiralised veg. Meanwhile, Babita finds out how were being manipulated by food health labels.

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: Marcus develops plans to create a orchard and bring in the perfect eating apple for his favourite dessert, the classic tarte Tatin. A trip to Kent introduces him to the wide range of heritage apples available in the UK, as well as giving him some tips and tricks on how to grow his own. Back at the farm, he gets a lesson on attracting pollinators, and discovers there is a whole world of edible flowers. He also cooks up some incredible Indian-style chutneys with a glut from the kitchen garden.

programme.desc: Whether savoury or sweet, the British love puddings. Dave and Si create some classic puds and explore the history and traditions behind our favourites. programme.title: Rick Steins Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: Rick is spoilt for choice as he explores the multifaceted food of Penang, an appealing mix of indigenous Malay, Indian and Chinese with a bit of British charm thrown in for good measure. programme.desc: The cars put to the test include the Aston Martin Rapide, the Porsche Panamera Turbo and the Maserati Quattroporte, in a search for the worlds greatest four seat supercar. programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return in an episode packed with some of the best bits from series 14. Over the course of the show, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Among the guests joining Lee and David are Richard Osman, Claudia Winkleman, Samantha Morton, AJ Odudu, Miles Jupp, Laura Whitmore, Lou Sanders, Chris McCausland, Sara Barron, Raj Bisram, Bob Mortimer and Sara Pascoe.

programme.desc: Guy trains to become a firefighter in some scarily real environments.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond finds out if Volkswagen has improved its second-generation Touareg 4x4 by taking it to Sweden for a race against some hardcore snowmobilers. Elsewhere, James May is in Germany attempting to break his personal speed record in a brand new, even more powerful version of the amazing Bugatti Veyron, and Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz are the Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May buy old British roadsters from Jensen-Healey, Lotus and TVR before taking a wistful journey through the remnants of the UKs sports car industry, starting in Norfolk and ending up at the mothballed TVR factory in Blackpool. Also, Jeremy takes to the test track in the sensational new Ferrari 458 Italia, and Hollywood legend Jeff Goldblum is the star in the new reasonably priced car.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: As an island, Britain is blessed with a plentiful and diverse supply of produce from the sea. Dave and Si celebrate our love of fish and shellfish and find out the history of Billingsgate Market.

programme.title: Rick Steins Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: Rick visits Sri Lanka and discovers a wealth of hot and spicy vegetable curries and then moves on to Bali, where food takes on a religious significance. He samples the best roast pork hes ever tasted.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon returns with an episode of previously unseen material from the current series. Among the guests joining Lee Mack and David Mitchell are Claudia Winkleman, Dan Walker, AJ Odudu, Samantha Morton, Miles Jupp, Roisin Conaty, Sara Barron, Chris McCausland, Gemma Cairney, Bob Mortimer, Sara Pascoe, Mr Motivator, Ed Gamble, Reverend Kate Bottley, Sophie Hermann, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Sindhu Vee.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants David James, Rhys James, Denise Lewis and Isy Suttie take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.desc: Dara O Briain and the team look back over another weeks events with guests Milton Jones, Miles Jupp and Josh Widdicombe.
