programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.sub-title: A Faraway Country About Which We Know Nothing
programme.desc: The enrapturing lives of those upstairs and downstairs in 165 Eaton Place are revisited. As 165 Eaton Place reopens its doors, two new arrivals make their mark. Whilst Sir Hallams forthright aunt Blanche disrupts life upstairs and feisty new maid Beryl struggles to adjust to life in service, Lady Agnes concern for her children reveals a dark secret about Mr Pritchard.

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.sub-title: The Love That Pays the Price
programme.desc: Lady Agnes catches the eye of charismatic American millionaire Caspar Landry but, as she and Sir Hallam look to their future, a tragic turn of events leaves their promised dreams - and a close family member - in jeopardy. Meanwhile, downstairs, Mrs Thackeray makes a momentous decision, which throws the running of the house into chaos, and Blanche and Mr Amanjit continue to clash.

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Blanche comes face to face with her former lover when a scandalous novel is published and, when the passion between the pair reignites, begins to consider a life away from Eaton Place. Downstairs, Beryls decision to take a stand causes serious ructions to the running of the house until a familiar face offers wise words.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: This edition features two couples who have stuck together despite the challenges life has thrown at them. Ryan started dating Keely when they were both at school, but to their shock Keely fell pregnant very quickly. Katie says she is the grown up in the relationship and her fiance Steve is like a child. In the delivery suite, Steve is on form constantly mucking about and generally driving Katie up the wall.

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Leah is having her sixth baby but unfortunately her partner is in prison and will miss the birth. Meanwhile, Becky met Police Sergeant John one Halloween when he was dressed as a witch and it was love at first sight. Within weeks they were engaged and trying for the child theyd both always wanted.

programme.title: Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Alex Henriquez, a successful businessman from the Bronx in New York City, was convicted of killing three girls but claims he is an innocent man. The three victims had two things in common - they were all asphyxiated before being dumped by the highway, and they could all be placed with Alex Henriquez shortly before their deaths. For nearly 30 years, Henriquez has protested his innocence claiming the police set him up.

programme.title: Confessions of a Serial Killer with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In Piers Morgans previous interviews with convicted serial killers, all protested their innocence. Now, for the first time, Piers comes face-face with a serial killer who is willing to admit to his heinous crimes. Over three months in late 1973, 20-year-old Bernard Giles picked up five innocent young women hitchhiking through his neighbourhood, and slaughtered each of them in cold blood. Fortunate to be spared the death penalty, he will never be released from prison.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers the story of the monks and villagers of the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, off the Northumberland coast. From Holy Island, monks spread Christianity to the rest of Northern England and created one of Britains finest treasures: The Lindisfarne Gospels.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson reveals the story of this Devon coastal gems transformation from fishing village to romantic Victorian seaside resort.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: The small hamlet of Botallack was once at the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry as archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers. By 1861, Botallacks mine was one of the biggest in the whole of Cornwall. Ben explores the villages mining past with archaeologist, Adam Sharpe, where he learns the mines produced millions and millions of pounds worth of copper and tin.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Sitting on the east coast of Jersey, and about 14 miles from the coast of Normandy, the small village of Gorey was, for hundreds of years the frontline of defence between the British Isles and its enemy, the French. Archaeologist Ben Robinson follows the story of this small villages history as an important military location, from the castle built in the 1200s to the experiences of the village in the second world war when occupied by Hitlers army.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves in Lincolnshire picking up a 1935 Riley Falcon - a good-looking saloon car, with a pre-select gearbox.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: When people think of Skoda, they think of cheap and efficient family cars. As early as the 1930s, their factory was a cover for espionage, sabotage and the liberation of Jews from all over Europe.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: At the end of March 1945, with the German Army utterly depleted and Hitlers circle of trust rapidly shrinking, Hitler invites a group of Hitler Youth to the Reich Chancellery gardens to be congratulated. These are the people upon whom he now relies, children.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At Piece Hall in Halifax, treasures include a royal portrait by Beryl Cook and artwork by the `Pennine Painter Peter Brook. Jewellery expert Susan Rumfitt admires an art deco bracelet, while John Benjamin marvels at a diamond necklace.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At Wrest Park in Bedfordshire, treasures include a piece of Murano glass inspired by Picasso and a collection of 1950s advertising posters. Expert Ronnie Archer-Morgan challenges the audience to guess the origins of three pieces of tribal jewellery.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business, which uncovers some remarkably rare vehicles in Thornton-le-Dale.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: When people think of Skoda, they think of cheap and efficient family cars. As early as the 1930s, their factory was a cover for espionage, sabotage and the liberation of Jews from all over Europe.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: At the end of March 1945, with the German Army utterly depleted and Hitlers circle of trust rapidly shrinking, Hitler invites a group of Hitler Youth to the Reich Chancellery gardens to be congratulated. These are the people upon whom he now relies, children.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At Piece Hall in Halifax, treasures include a royal portrait by Beryl Cook and artwork by the `Pennine Painter Peter Brook. Jewellery expert Susan Rumfitt admires an art deco bracelet, while John Benjamin marvels at a diamond necklace.

programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley investigates the dark history of our curious relationship with killing, beginning with notorious real-life crimes of the first half of the nineteenth century, and how these murders were transformed into popular entertainments.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Situated on the eastern coast of the Isle of Man, the village of Laxey is known today as a tourist destination, but as archaeologist Ben Robinson finds out, it wasnt the beaches and the mountains that attracted Victorian tourists, but an engineering marvel - the biggest waterwheel in the world.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson takes us to the quiet village of Ravenglass, tucked away on the edge of the Lake Districts National Park, where he finds out about its strategic importance to the Roman empire. Ben explores the village and its surrounding area to uncover the remarkable evidence of its Roman past: unearthing Roman pottery, locating the earthworks of its fort and marvelling at one the best surviving examples of a Roman military bathhouse anywhere in Britain.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist, Ben Robinson visits the east coast village of Flamborough which sits on a rugged headland sticking out into the North Sea. A largely unassuming village today, it was once effectively cut off from the rest of the country by a five-metre-deep, two-and-a-half-mile long man-made dyke, re-enforced and fortified with local chalk. As Ben discovers when he visits two of the villages most prominent structures, chalk plays a large part in the history of Flamborough.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea and discovers that due to Dutch settlers in the Elizabethan era, its not as next the sea as it once was. Ben finds a village green that was once a thriving port with big ships and surrounded by a thriving village. He visits the church and meets Matt Champion, an expert in medieval graffiti who has found some incredible links to past villagers.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek brings a small army of military hardware to Thornton le Dale including a Studebaker Weasel tracked vehicle and a WWII-era Willys Jeep.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Ferdinand Porsche was one of the most influential engineers in Nazi Germany. By inventing the most powerful war machines, he created the basis for the muscle cars that made his name famous.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: In April 1945, the Nazis organise one of the final acts of the Third Reich, a concert by the Berlin Philharmonic, including the last scene of the opera Die Gotterdammerung, which features a suicide at its centre. Its a clear sign that Hitler and many of his supporters are going to end it all.

programme.title: American Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley reveals the historic myths and deceptions told following the United States emergence as a superpower after the Second World War. The 1950s and early 1960s in America are often remembered as a golden era of abundance, harmony and the American dream made real. This film reveals that to be a carefully constructed illusion.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers the story of the monks and villagers of the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, off the Northumberland coast. From Holy Island, monks spread Christianity to the rest of Northern England and created one of Britains finest treasures: The Lindisfarne Gospels.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson reveals the story of this Devon coastal gems transformation from fishing village to romantic Victorian seaside resort.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: The small hamlet of Botallack was once at the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry as archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers. By 1861, Botallacks mine was one of the biggest in the whole of Cornwall. Ben explores the villages mining past with archaeologist, Adam Sharpe, where he learns the mines produced millions and millions of pounds worth of copper and tin.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Sitting on the east coast of Jersey, and about 14 miles from the coast of Normandy, the small village of Gorey was, for hundreds of years the frontline of defence between the British Isles and its enemy, the French. Archaeologist Ben Robinson follows the story of this small villages history as an important military location, from the castle built in the 1200s to the experiences of the village in the second world war when occupied by Hitlers army.

programme.title: Pure Bollywood

programme.title: Crook
programme.desc: After a violent turn of events, a career criminal finally gets a shot at the big time. One of the biggest crime bosses in the city wants him to get rid of a little problem: a man who could put him and many of his associates away for a long time.

programme.title: Ek Aur Ek Gyarah
programme.desc: Petty thieves Tara and Sitara get on the bad side of two wanted criminals, namely Panther and Cobra. On the run, they end up at the home of Major Ram Singh, who takes them in, but is eventually betrayed by them.

programme.title: Awarapan
programme.desc: A hit-man falls in love with a devout Muslim woman who opens his eyes to compassion and the teachings of Pighamber Mohammad.

programme.title: Hum Saath Saath Hain
programme.desc: Ramkishen and his wife Mamta have three sons. Prem and Vinod have sweethearts in Preeti and Vivek marries Sangeeta. Since Vivek is Mamtas step-son, she plots to alienate him, but will she succeed?

programme.title: Attack
programme.desc: Manu has the date 11 March 2019 engraved in his memory, as it was the day his father died. Seeking revenge, his path intersects with Monica, for whom the same date holds great importance, and the two lives inevitably collide on a single street.

programme.title: Prem Ratan Dhan Payo
programme.desc: A charitable, happy-go-lucky man embarks on a mission to meet a generous princess who helps people, so he takes the place of a prince.

programme.title: Jai Ho
programme.desc: A solitary man wages a tireless battle to save ordinary people from the ever-present threats of corruption and injustice.

programme.title: Chintu Ka Birthday
programme.desc: The US invasion of Iraq where amidst all the danger, an Indian family awaiting its return to India struggles to celebrate the birthday of its youngest member.

programme.title: Shakti - The Power
programme.desc: A woman goes to visit her in-laws and finds out that her father-in-law is a gangster. She has to find a way of escaping her father-in-law to get herself and her son back home.

programme.title: Holiday: A Soldier Is Never Off Duty
programme.desc: After a bomb destroys the bus on which he was riding, a military officer foregoes his vacation to hunt down a terrorist and the sleeper cells under his command.

programme.title: Maine Pyar Kiya
programme.desc: Prem loves Suman but to marry her, he has to prove to her father that he is not the same as his own dad.

programme.title: Batti Gul Meter Chalu
programme.desc: A lawyer takes up the case when an electricity company massively increases prices.

programme.title: Coolie No. 1
programme.desc: After being insulted by a rich businessman, pandit Jai Kishen teaches him a lesson by getting his daughter married to Raju, a coolie posing as a millionaire.

programme.title: Radhe: Your Most Wanted Bhai
programme.desc: After taking the dreaded gangster Gani Bhai, ACP Rajveer Shikawat, as known as Radhe, goes on a manhunt to find the wealthiest man of the town secretly running a crime syndicate.

programme.title: Krrish
programme.desc: Sequel to `Koi... Mil Gaya, continuing the story of Rohit Mehra, a young man who has inherited supernatural powers thanks to his fathers encounter with an alien. The film was Indian cinemas biggest hit in 2006, with special effects and stunts that stole the hearts of millions of Indians.
